{"problem_standardized_math":"Prove the second part of Theorem 17.4: if \\(\\hat{x}\\) is a stationary point of \\(\\phi_1(x;\\mu)\\) for all sufficiently large \\(\\mu\\), but \\(\\hat{x}\\) is infeasible for problem \\((17.6)\\), then \\(\\hat{x}\\) is an infeasible stationary point. Here\n\\[\nh(x)=\\sum_{i\\in\\mathcal{E}} |c_i(x)|+\\sum_{i\\in\\mathcal{I}} [c_i(x)]^{-}.\n\\]\nHint: use the identity\n\\[\nD(\\phi_1(\\hat{x};\\mu);p)=\\nabla f(\\hat{x})^T p+\\mu\\,D(h(\\hat{x});p).\n\\]"}
